speaker: Andre Agassi
verification_notes: Created 2026-05-26 via the Six-State Buildout (Phase 8 human-gated promotion). prototype_only reconstruction — paraphrase of the speaker's documented idea; NOT a verified verbatim excerpt. Andre Agassi is the only player to have won all four Grand Slams, a gold medal, and the ATP World Tour Finals. "Open" was a #1 NYT bestseller, won the William Hill Sports Book of the Year, and is widely cited in discussions of athletic identity. Tier-2 credibility (elite athlete, acclaimed memoir). The book is real and available through Penguin/Random House. Source verified via Amazon official listing, multiple book reviews, and Wikipedia. The core claim — that Agassi hated tennis yet it organized his identity, and that retirement created an identity vacuum — is confirmed by multiple independent reviews of the book and by a 2016 CNN interview ("Agassi: prospect of retiring 'is like preparing for death'"). Paraphrase: source is a book; no transcript to fetch.
rights_or_usage_notes: Source-link to the canonical official page. Brief paraphrase for attribution; do not reproduce extended passages or re-host media.
